what: Critical design constraint: single-BGP SPARQL discipline
title: AGENTS.md
text: librdf C library rdflib wraps serious correctness performance bugs conjunctive triple patterns graph’s scale. Multi-pattern SPARQL queries: Joins via shared variables (?s P1 ?o1. ?s P2 ?o2) hang many minutes. Multi-property selects single subject silently return zero rows. Single basic graph patterns (one triple match per query) execute correctly milliseconds. package’s design discipline therefore: SPARQL queries single basic graph patterns. Joins, multi-property assembly, aggregation happen R via dplyr. implemented R/sparql-helpers.R. two primitives (sparql_pairs, sparql_subjects) issue exactly one triple match; four domain helpers (framework_metadata, role_framework_bindings, element_framework_bindings, role_element_bindings) compose multiple single-BGP calls join R. , agent, asked write multi-pattern SPARQL query package’s graphs: don’t. Decompose single-BGP calls join dplyr. future v0.2+ release may add Apache Jena Fuseki backend lifts constraint.

title: Adding a New Framework to cybedtools
text: scripts/020-assemble-jsonld.R, add assembler function register : assembly_config$frameworks, add "frameworkx" loop picks .
code: assemble_frameworkx <- function() {   # Read the manifest and tidy CSVs your ingestion script wrote.   prov  <- load_framework_provenance("frameworkx")   roles <- read_framework_table("frameworkx", "roles")   elements <- read_framework_table("frameworkx", "elements")    # Build the framework-level JSON-LD node. Subclasses cybed:Framework via   # the fx: prefix; downstream SPARQL queries match on cybed:Framework   # and so include this framework automatically.   framework_node <- build_framework_node(     framework_id     = "frameworkx-v1",     framework_name   = prov$framework_version,     framework_prefix = "fx",     version          = prov$framework_version,     publisher        = prov$source$publisher,     jurisdiction     = "US",     sector           = "civilian",     specificity      = "cybersecurity-specific",     license          = prov$licensing$source_license,     date_published   = prov$framework_date   )    # One JSON-LD node per role. Each subclasses cybed:Role and is bound to   # the framework via cybed:partOf in build_role_node().   role_nodes <- roles |>     purrr::pmap(function(role_id, role_name, ...) {       build_role_node(         role_id              = role_id,         role_name            = role_name,         framework_prefix     = "fx",         framework_role_type  = "WorkRole",         framework_id         = "frameworkx-v1"       )     })    # One JSON-LD node per atomic element. Each subclasses cybed:RoleElement   # so cross-framework SPARQL queries pick them up alongside NICE tasks,   # SFIA skill levels, CSEC2017 essentials, and the rest.   element_nodes <- elements |>     purrr::pmap(function(element_id, text, ...) {       build_role_element_node(         element_id             = element_id,         framework_prefix       = "fx",         framework_element_type = "Element",         element_text           = text,         framework_id           = "frameworkx-v1"       )     })    # The orchestrator collects framework + role + element nodes and the   # prefix; it then assembles the per-framework JSON-LD document and   # merges it into the combined graph.   list(framework = framework_node, roles = role_nodes, elements = element_nodes,        prefix = "fx") }  # Register the assembler so the orchestrator can find it. framework_assemblers[["frameworkx"]] <- assemble_frameworkx framework_to_prefix[["frameworkx"]]  <- "fx"

title: Cross-framework Analysis with cybedtools
code: # Compare role density: NICE (role-first specification) vs SFIA (skill-first). rfb <- role_framework_bindings(rdf) reb <- role_element_bindings(rdf)  # Filter to the two frameworks of interest, attach element counts to each # role, then aggregate role-count and element-density statistics per # framework. Useful when arguing about how differently the two structure # their workforce specifications. rfb |>   filter(framework_name %in% c("NICE v2 (NIST SP 800-181 Rev 1 components)", "SFIA 9")) |>   left_join(reb |> count(role, name = "element_count"), by = "role") |>   group_by(framework_name) |>   summarize(     role_count      = n(),     mean_elements   = round(mean(element_count, na.rm = TRUE), 1),     median_elements = median(element_count, na.rm = TRUE),     max_elements    = max(element_count, na.rm = TRUE)   )

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Every framework must provenance.yml recording: Framework name version. Source publisher, type, retrieval URL (manual acquisition note). SHA256 canonical source artifact (XLSX, JSON, SQLite, PDF). File size bytes. Retrieval date (ISO-8601) retriever identity (script path). Licensing terms redistribution constraints. Check: provenance.yml exists, parses YAML, contains required keys, recorded SHA256 matches file disk.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Every framework must declare expected entity counts docs/framework-invariants.yml. Actual extraction counts must fall within tolerance band. Example: Check: Every ingested framework expected counts declared. Actual counts fall inside declared ranges. Tolerance bands justified inline comments. Rule: actual counts fall outside expected ranges, ingestion flagged potential source revision rather silently accepted. Human review updates expected bounds.
code: nice:   version: "v2"   expected:     work_roles: [41, 41]     unique_tks: [2109, 2113] sfia:   version: "SFIA 9"   expected:     skills: [145, 149]     skill_levels: [670, 675]

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Every within-framework identifier reference must resolve. Examples: DCWF per-role sheets reference Task KSA IDs must exist master catalog. ECSF e-competence references must match known e-CF code pattern. SFIA SkillLevel rows reference Skill codes must exist Skill table (enforced foreign keys SQLite, verified post-extraction). NICE role-TKS associations reference work_role_id (must exist work-roles.csv) statement_id (must exist tasks.csv, knowledge.csv, skills.csv). Check: declared cross-reference, unresolved references enumerated reported. Zero unresolved pass criterion. Current implementation status: Full referential-integrity automation progress. Partial coverage exists via foreign-key constraints source format (SFIA SQLite). Remaining gaps caught implicitly extraction-count bounds (catch structural drift) SPARQL queries (silently drop unresolved references, graph broken refs shows visible element-count shortfalls query results). full explicit check roadmap.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Statement text must pass: UTF-8 validity. replacement characters (U+FFFD), unexpected encoding artifacts. Non-empty. Empty strings element-text columns errors, data. Length sanity. Statements approximately 10 characters approximately 5,000 characters flagged manual review (likely extraction errors merged cells). truncation suspicious round numbers. every statement happens exactly 255 characters, indicates silent column truncation upstream. Check: flagged conditions produce error output. Nothing silently accepted.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Within framework, every element role ID unique. Across frameworks, element IDs must namespaced framework prefix (e.g., nice:OG-WRL-015-T01, bare T01). JSON-LD assembly enforces . verification rig confirms . Check: duplicate IDs within framework. IDs assembled graphs carry framework prefix.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Re-running ingestion script source file must produce byte-identical tidy CSVs. means: timestamps environment-varying data embedded output files. Deterministic row ordering (sort stable key write). Fixed locale number date formatting. Check: Run ingestion twice diff outputs. Zero delta pass criterion. (Exception: provenance.yml retrieval_date differs excluded diff.)

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Every ingestion verification run writes entry data/audit/audit-log.ndjson: Timestamp (ISO-8601 timezone). Script path (version control) git commit hash. SHA256 output file. Pass/fail invariant. Check: audit log append-. Gaps rewrites errors.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: every ingestion script. scripts/010-ingest-*.R exits non-zero status verification fails. pre-analysis gate. scripts/020+ scripts refuse run audit log shows recent failure. pre-distribution gate. output artifact built pipeline released verification failing.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Hard failures (block downstream work): Missing invalid provenance.yml. SHA256 mismatch source file. Unresolved within-framework references. Duplicate IDs. Invalid UTF-8 empty required text fields. Soft flags (warn allow, written justification): Extraction count outside expected range (indicates source revision, human review required next run). Statements outside length sanity band (likely merged cells truncation, human review).

title: Data integrity protocol
text: verification fails, response never quietly adjust data pass. response : Investigate root cause. Update protocol (invariant wrong) fix source/extraction (data wrong). Document fix audit log human sign-. Re-run.

title: Getting Started with cybedtools

title: JSON-LD namespace architecture
text: architecture uses two-tier namespace structure: base vocabulary cross-framework, framework-specific vocabularies subclass base. tier distinct prefix. base vocabulary subsumes conceptual common ground -scope frameworks: framework, role, role element (task, knowledge statement, skill statement, competence, learning standard), element text, source reference, structural metadata (jurisdiction, sector, specificity). Per-framework vocabularies specialize via subclassing, single SPARQL query targeting cybed:RoleElement returns elements across NICE, DCWF, e-CF, SFIA, pedagogical frameworks one pass. Schema.org (schema:) SKOS (skos:) provide outermost vocabulary layer generic properties name, description, prefLabel, altLabel. project namespace URI https://w3id.org/cybed/ontology# (prefix cybed). w3id.org provides stable, redirectable URIs vocabularies via community-maintained GitHub repository, means URI persists across changes maintainer’s personal infrastructure. what: Why a custom URI rather than reusing existing ontologies
title: JSON-LD namespace architecture
text: natural semantic-web question : mint cybed: rather reuse existing competency skills ontology? Three constraints made reuse insufficient. Coverage. Existing competency ontologies (ESCO, O*NET, CEDEFOP qualifications taxonomies) cover labor-market competencies general terms represent structural commitments specific frameworks package handles. NICE work roles, SFIA skill levels, ENISA ECSF role profiles carry framework-specific structural metadata (TKS classifications, responsibility levels, cross-framework references) ESCO-based mapping either lose distort. Pedagogical-frameworks gap. ESCO workforce-competency ontologies represent K-12 higher-education curricular frameworks (Cyber.org K-12 standards, CSTA standards, CSEC2017 guidelines, DigComp). package’s design commitment make workforce frameworks pedagogical frameworks queryable schema. requires base vocabulary subsumes , existing competency ontologies . Comparison-layer purpose. cybed: schema designed specifically cross-framework comparison: single SPARQL query targeting cybed:RoleElement returns elements across every framework one pass, regardless whether element NICE TaskStatement, SFIA SkillLevel, CSEC2017 Essential. Existing ontologies built subsume heterogeneity comparison layer. existing vocabulary fits cleanly, schema reuses : Schema.org (schema:) generic properties (name, description, version, publisher, datePublished, license) SKOS (skos:) prefLabel altLabel. Tier 1 cybed: types specialize framework structure requires . Tier 2 framework prefixes specialize cybed: types framework vocabulary requires .

what: Why single-BGP queries with R-side joins
title: SPARQL query strategy
text: librdf C library rdflib wraps exhibits poor performance silent zero-row results conjunctive triple patterns graph’s scale. Multi-pattern SPARQL joins via shared variables hang many minutes. Multi-property selects single subject silently return rows. Single basic graph patterns (one triple match per SPARQL call) execute fast correctly. package’s discipline therefore: SPARQL queries single basic graph patterns. One triple match per call. Joins, multi-property assembly, aggregation happen R via dplyr. implemented helpers R/sparql-helpers.R: sparql_pairs(rdf, predicate) returns subject-object pairs triples given predicate. sparql_subjects(rdf, predicate, object) returns subjects triples whose predicate object fixed. Domain-level helpers compose primitives: framework_metadata(rdf) returns tibble (framework, name, jurisdiction, sector, specificity). role_framework_bindings(rdf) returns (role, role_name, framework, framework_name). element_framework_bindings(rdf) returns (element, framework, framework_name). role_element_bindings(rdf) returns (role, element). domain helper makes one four single-BGP queries joins results via dplyr left-joins semi-joins.

title: cybedtools 0.1.0
text: Initial public release. Eight cybersecurity workforce learning frameworks (NICE, DCWF, ECSF, SFIA, Cyber.org K-12, CSTA, ACM/IEEE CSEC2017, DigComp 2.2) ingested shared two-tier JSON-LD schema queryable via single-BGP SPARQL R-side joins.
